evals.name: consumer-hot-sauce
evals.prompt: I'm launching a small-batch artisanal hot sauce brand. We ferment everything for 6+ months using locally-sourced peppers from farms in the Willamette Valley. Our sauces range from a mild smoky chipotle to a face-melting Carolina Reaper blend. Target audience is food enthusiasts aged 25-45 who shop at farmers' markets, specialty grocery stores, and follow food creators on TikTok/Instagram. The brand should feel: craft, authentic, bold, earthy, with a bit of irreverence. We want something distinctive - not another 'fire' or 'burn' name. Think more Fly By Jing energy than Frank's RedHot. Main competitors: Yellowbird, Secret Aardvark, Heatonist, Fly By Jing, Cholula, Truff. I'd love a name that works on a bottle label and as an Instagram handle.
evals.expected_output: Full generation + riffing output with consumer-specific techniques applied

assertions.text: At least 6 of 11 naming categories attempted
Five Senses Scan run as a dedicated generation pass (not inline)
Sensory Dissonance (Cowshed Strategy) applied or offered
Emotional Temperature Mapping referenced or applied
Ingredient/Process Vocabulary Mining applied (fermentation terms)
Cultural Tension Naming explored
Arbitrary/Absurd Association angle explored
Ownability-first compounds generated in parallel (B2B briefs) OR compound track correctly skipped per SKILL.md guidance (consumer/fashion/food briefs)
Compound elements generated dynamically from the brief (not static suffix list)
Did NOT default to B2B compound patterns (-works, -craft, -wise) for this consumer brief
At least 3 structural types represented (found words, compounds, coined, blends, etc.)
Tree of Thought branching used (3 parallel mini-batches per category)
Each riff labeled with which technique produced it
10+ NEW UNIQUE riffs per direction (duplicates don't count), OR 8+ per direction if total riff count exceeds 60
Linguistic riffing applied (morpheme swap, language shift, etymology mining)
Conceptual riffing applied (zoom in/out, adjacent metaphor, sensory shift)
Structural riffing applied (syllable reduction, compound creation, blend)
Consumer riffing checklist used (adjacent metaphor, sensory expansion, nature mining, French naturalized vocab) NOT B2B checklist
Hard gate checks displayed before generation menu (count, categories, structural mix, technique cycling)
Inline conflict flagging for known conflicts (⚠️ emoji or text flag — substance over format)
Gallery mode presentation used — scannable grid/table format for category overview (H/W/C reaction prompts in interactive sessions, structured table in automated sessions)
At least 40 unique candidates generated
Shelf Test or Gift Test referenced for consumer context
Social-native testing considered (hashtag, handle, caption tests)
Technique toolkit offered at checkpoint with applied/unapplied status and user selection option

evals.name: consumer-streetwear
evals.prompt: I'm starting a sustainable streetwear brand. We use deadstock fabrics and recycled materials to make limited-run drops of hoodies, tees, and accessories. Based in East London. Target audience: 18-30 year olds who care about sustainability but don't want to look like they shop at a hemp store. Think the intersection of Stussy and Patagonia. The vibe is: rebellious, conscious, urban, a bit dark, anti-corporate. Competitors in our space: Pangaia, Story MFG, 69 Worldwide, Brain Dead, Stussy. The name needs to work as a streetwear label - something you'd see on a tag or embroidered on a chest. Preferably one word or short.
evals.expected_output: Full generation + riffing output with consumer/fashion-specific techniques applied

assertions.text: At least 6 of 11 naming categories attempted
Five Senses Scan run (fashion is a physical product)
Subculture Code-Switch Naming applied (streetwear/skate codes)
Arbitrary/Absurd Association heavily weighted (per SKILL.md fashion guidance)
Contrarian/Anti-Category Naming applied
B2B compound track SKIPPED per SKILL.md fashion guidance ('Nobody in streetwear wears Loamworks')
Heavy found word, sensory, and arbitrary emphasis (per fashion playbook)
Badgeability/Wearability test applied (would you wear this on a hat?)
Cultural Tension Naming explored (sustainability vs streetwear tension)
Desired-Self Naming considered (what identity does buying this project?)
Trend-Cycle Audit applied (will this name date?)
Each riff labeled with which technique produced it
10+ NEW UNIQUE riffs per direction (duplicates don't count), OR 8+ per direction if total riff count exceeds 60
Consumer riffing checklist used (adjacent metaphor, sensory expansion, nature mining, French naturalized vocab)
Hard gate checks displayed before generation menu
Inline conflict flagging for known conflicts (⚠️ emoji or text flag — substance over format)
Relaxed conflict-flagging per fashion guidance (a plumbing company conflict is irrelevant)
At least 40 unique candidates generated
Place/Environment Evocation explored (East London)
Memetic/Viral Engineering considered
Technique toolkit offered at checkpoint with applied/unapplied status and user selection option

evals.name: consumer-mens-skincare
evals.prompt: I'm developing a premium men's skincare line targeting men aged 28-45 who are just getting into skincare. Not the Axe Body Spray guy - more the guy who reads GQ, owns a nice watch, and is willing to spend $40-60 on a moisturizer but would never walk into Sephora. Products: cleanser, moisturizer, eye cream, SPF. The brand should feel: elevated but masculine, clinical-meets-luxury, no-nonsense, confident. Think Aesop meets The Ordinary but specifically for men. Don't want anything too feminine (Glossier-ish), too aggressive (War Paint), or too clinical (CeraVe). Competitors: Lumin, Kiehl's, Bulldog, Harry's (skincare line), Hims. We're DTC-first with plans for Nordstrom/Saks placement. The name should look good on a matte black bottle.
evals.expected_output: Full generation + riffing output with consumer/beauty-specific techniques applied

assertions.text: At least 6 of 11 naming categories attempted
Five Senses Scan run as dedicated generation pass (skincare is sensory)
Emotional Temperature Mapping applied (masc positioning)
Price-Signal Calibration applied (luxury/masstige register)
Adjacent-Category Code Borrowing explored (e.g., spirits/fragrance codes for skincare)
Front-of-Pack Hierarchy Fit tested (name on a matte black bottle)
Dual-audience consideration (men who want skincare but masculine framing)
Desired-Self Naming explored (what identity does this project?)
Color-to-Name Mapping explored (matte black palette)
At least 3 structural types represented
Ownability alternatives generated alongside found words
Compound vocabulary dynamically generated from beauty/skincare domain (not tech suffixes)
Each riff labeled with which technique produced it
10+ NEW UNIQUE riffs per direction (duplicates don't count), OR 8+ per direction if total riff count exceeds 60
Consumer riffing checklist used (not B2B checklist)
Hard gate checks displayed before generation menu
Inline conflict flagging for known conflicts (⚠️ emoji or text flag — substance over format)
At least 40 unique candidates generated
Shelf Test applied (Nordstrom/Saks shelf context)
Gift Test considered (premium skincare is highly giftable)
French Naturalized Vocabulary Mining applied (premium/luxury register)
Sound symbolism considered (angular sounds for masculine positioning)
Technique toolkit offered at checkpoint with applied/unapplied status and user selection option

evals.name: enterprise-cybersecurity
evals.prompt: We're building an AI-powered cybersecurity platform that uses ML to detect network anomalies, automate incident response, and provide continuous compliance monitoring. We just raised a $12M Series A. Target customers: CISOs and security teams at mid-to-large enterprises (500-10000 employees). Should feel authoritative, technically credible, modern but not gimmicky. We want engineers to respect the name AND C-suite to feel comfortable putting it in a board deck. Competitors: CrowdStrike, SentinelOne, Darktrace, Wiz, Orca Security, Palo Alto Networks. We're positioning as the next-gen platform — AI-native, not bolted-on AI. Name should work in 'Gartner names [X] a Leader in the CNAPP Magic Quadrant' and also as a CLI command.
evals.expected_output: Full generation + riffing output with enterprise/cybersecurity-specific techniques applied

assertions.text: At least 6 of 11 naming categories attempted
Vertical Code Calibration applied for cybersecurity
Buyer Committee test applied (champion, CFO, technical evaluator, end users)
Sales Conversation Fit templates tested (elevator pitch, board deck, RFP, Slack, phone)
Analyst/Press Headline Testing applied (Gartner, Forbes)
Enterprise Objection Preemption applied (CISO test, resume test)
Developer/Technical Credibility Signaling considered (CLI-friendly)
Acronym Resilience tested on finalists
Trust-Without-Claims Lexicon used (avoid 'secure', 'shield', 'protect')
Governance/Audit Vocabulary Mining applied
Ownability-first compounds generated in parallel track
Tech Branding Patterns applied (-works, -craft, -wise, etc.)
Institutional Compound patterns explored (Color+Noun, Quality+Noun)
Lexicon Three-Team Method applied during generation or riffing
Each riff labeled with which technique produced it
10+ NEW UNIQUE riffs per direction (duplicates don't count), OR 8+ per direction if total riff count exceeds 60
B2B riffing checklist used (SCAMPER, compound creation, creative respelling, tech branding)
Hard gate checks displayed before generation menu
Inline conflict flagging for known conflicts (⚠️ emoji or text flag — substance over format)
At least 40 unique candidates generated
Platform vs Point-Solution naming considered (this is a platform)
Stack test with VISIBLE per-name output showing names alongside Salesforce, AWS, etc. (not just claimed)
Technique toolkit offered at checkpoint with applied/unapplied status and user selection option

evals.name: enterprise-data-integration
evals.prompt: We're building a data integration middleware platform that connects enterprise systems - think Salesforce, Snowflake, Databricks, SAP - with a no-code visual builder and AI-powered data mapping. Series B, $30M raised. Target: data engineering teams and IT leaders at enterprises with 50+ SaaS tools. Should feel: technical but accessible, reliable, enterprise-grade. The core value prop is interoperability - we're the connective tissue. Competitors: MuleSoft, Boomi, Fivetran, Airbyte, Workato, Tray.io. Name needs to work in 'Powered by [X]' and '[X] for Salesforce' integration grammar. Developer audience skews technical - they'll judge us on the name.
evals.expected_output: Full generation + riffing output with enterprise/integration-specific techniques applied

Buyer Committee test applied

evals.name: enterprise-legaltech
evals.prompt: We're building an AI-powered legal document review platform. We use LLMs to analyze contracts, flag risks, extract key terms, and compare against playbooks. Seed stage, $4M raised. Target: legal operations teams and in-house counsel at companies with 10+ lawyers. Should feel authoritative and precise, but NOT stuffy Latin (no Lexicon, no Juris, no Veritas - those are played out). Think more Clio/Relativity energy. This is a regulated industry - can't use words like 'attorney', 'counsel', 'legal advice'. Competitors: Kira Systems, eBrevia, Luminance, Ironclad, ContractPodAi, Harvey. The name should signal 'built for lawyers' without sounding like a law firm. Needs to work internationally - UK and EU markets from day one.
evals.expected_output: Full generation + riffing output with enterprise/legaltech-specific techniques applied

assertions.text: At least 6 of 11 naming categories attempted
Vertical Code Calibration applied for legaltech
Regulated-Industry Lexical Compliance applied (avoided 'attorney', 'counsel', 'legal advice')
Avoided transparent Latin clichés per user constraint AND skill guidance (Lex-, Juris-, Veritas-)
Buyer Committee test applied during shortlisting (Step 03a) OR offered in technique toolkit
Sales Conversation Fit templates tested during shortlisting OR offered in technique toolkit
Enterprise Objection Preemption applied during shortlisting OR offered in technique toolkit
Cross-language risk noted during generation (basic flags) OR systematic checks offered in technique toolkit
Ownability-first compounds generated in parallel track
Classical Stem Engineering applied (but naturalized, not transparent Latin per cliché guard)
Personified Names explored (Harvey is a competitor - this angle is proven in legaltech)
Lexicon Three-Team Method applied OR offered in technique toolkit
Each riff labeled with SPECIFIC technique (e.g., 'language shift: Latin', not just 'riff')
10+ NEW UNIQUE riffs per direction (duplicates don't count), OR 8+ per direction if total riff count exceeds 60
Homonym/Synonym/Sound-Alike Riffing applied OR offered in technique toolkit
B2B riffing checklist used
Hard gate checks displayed before generation menu
Inline conflict flagging for known conflicts (⚠️ emoji or text flag — substance over format)
At least 40 unique candidates generated

Technique toolkit offered at checkpoint with applied/unapplied status and user selection option
